title: PBP Weaponry
body:
![Peanut Butter Pretzels](https://cdn.modrinth.com/data/cached_images/df356d4a4800a0848b9a57c70df3918df2103415.png)

**Hi! I'm Ben. I make mods for you guys to play, because quite frankly why not? Enjoy Peanut Butter Pretzels, 'cuz I know I do!**

This mod has no specific theme, but it is an item expansion mod (mostly due to the fact I don't know how to add entities/mobs just quite yet) . Adds over 250 items, including music discs! (Music entirely made by Mercia & The Midlands, my awesome older brother.) Future updates to this mod will be coming out periodically!

<details>
<summary>Tool Progression</summary>
<details>
<summary>Vanilla</summary>

**New Tools**- For every base mineral resource, the cutlass (a slower, more powerful sword) and the katana (a quicker, less powerful sword) and the multitool (a pickaxe, axe, and shovel combined into one) are added!

**Wood**- Wooden tools now have the wooden katana, cutlass, and the wooden multitool, along with the wooden shield, and the wooden vest.

**Stone**- Stone tools now have the stone katana, cutlass, and the stone multitool, along with the stone shield, and the stone vest.

**Iron**- Iron tools now have the iron katana, cutlass, and the iron multitool.

**Gold**- Gold tools now have the gold katana, cutlass, and the gold multitool.

**Diamond**- Diamond tools now have the diamond katana, cutlass, and the diamond multitool.

<details>
<summary>Diamond Upgrades</summary>

Diamond now has a secondary progression system! You can now create diamond alloys with copper, iron, gold, and emerald to create stronger mini-levels of diamond before reaching netherite. But don't fret! Any stage of diamond can be upgraded into netherite at any time.

</details>

**Netherite**- Netherite tools now have the netherite katana, cutlass, and the netherite multitool.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Copper Path</summary>

Copper now has a use! (This was made before the copper age drop, don't flame me). You can now create a full set of copper tools and armor, including a shield! Copper rests right between stone and iron in the vanilla tool progression.

In the depths of the nether, you can now find a new type of ore! Tin! Tin can be used to make a full set of tools and armor, not including a shield. Tin falls between iron and diamond in the vanilla tool progression.

Additionally, using copper, tin and steel (blast an iron ingot in a blast furnace), you can upgrade tin tools and armor into bronze tools and armor in a smithing table! Bronze falls between tin and diamond.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Resin Path</summary>

For the few who know about resin and how to get it, you're in luck! You can now create a full set of resin tools and armor. Resin by itself rests between wood and stone, and if you wear the full set of resin armor you get a new potion effect; sap, which causes you to jump shorter and move and attack slower.

You can create a new alloy out of resin and gold- amber. Amber tools are made by upgrading resin tools with an amber ingot in a smithing table. Amber tools fall between tin and bronze.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Quartz and Blackstone</summary>

<details>
<summary>Quartz</summary>

Quartz can now be used to craft a full set of tools and armor. Quartz falls between copper and iron.

Quartz tools and armor can be upgraded with gilded quartz, an alloy created by quartz and gold. Gilded quartz falls between bronze and diamond.

Gilded quartz tools and armor can be upgraded with ruby, a new ore found in the nether. Ruby Quartz falls between diamond and netherite.

Ruby quartz tools and armor can be upgraded with a petrified nether star, a new powerful item crafted with a nether star and a casing created by ruby quartz ingots and netherite. Star Quartz tools are far better than Netherite.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Blackstone</summary>

Blackstone can now be used to craft a full set of tools and armor. Blackstone falls between copper and iron.

Blackstone tools and armor can be upgraded with gilded blackstone, an alloy created by blackstone and gold. Gilded blackstone falls between bronze and diamond.

Gilded blackstone tools and armor can be upgraded with ruby, a new ore found in the nether. Ruby blackstone falls between diamond and netherite.

Ruby blackstone tools and armor can be upgraded with a petrified nether star, a new powerful item crafted with a nether star and a casing created by ruby blackstone ingots and netherite. Star blackstone tools are far better than Netherite.

</details>

</details>

</details>


<details>
<summary>Biomes</summary>

**Ochre Forest**- A new forest found on the coast in humid, warm biomes with a new type of tree and wood, which comes in a beautiful yellow color! The biome is decored like a lush cave up on the surface!

**Verdant Dunes**- A new type of desert with a new type of tree and wood, which comes in a nice soft green color! The biome has a new type of sand covering it, etch, which can be dried and mined into a little statue.

**No Man's Land**- A void black biome found in the nether, complete with wither skeletons, black trees, and black wood.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Moon Minerals</summary>


<details>
<summary>Steel</summary>

Steel isn't only used for bronze; steel is also used to make a steel hammer! You all know the deal, pickaxe but a 3x3x1. It can also be used to make a steel plated shield.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Novalite</summary>

Novalite is a new ore found on the moon, and it is used to upgrade your steel hammer and shield to novalite tier.

</details>


<details>
<summary>Ancient Gold</summary>

Ancient gold is a new ore found on the moon, and it is used to upgrade your novalite hammer and shield to ancient gold tier.


<details>
<summary>Totems</summary>

Ancient gold is also useful for other things. You can use it to craft totems! First, you want to blast smelt any soul-fused block (soul sand, soul soil, or whisp soil) into a pure soul block. Then, surround the pure soul block with ancient gold clumps to get petrified souls. Then, free the souls from the block to get a pure soul. After this, surround a nether star with eight pure souls to get a genesis star. Then, surround the genesis star in the crafting table with eight ancient gold clumps to get eight blank totems! You can now use different minerals found in the world to create a totem of undying and four new totems!

**Totem of Flamewalking-** The totem of flamewalking (as long as you are holding it), not only protects you from fire or lava damage, but it actually heals you and gives you a damage boost as long as you are standing in fire or lava!

**Totem of the Nautilus-** The totem of the Nautilus gives you infinite water breathing, increased water speed, and increased underwater mining speed.

**Totem of Cowardice-** The totem of Cowardice grants you increased attack speed, attack knockback, attack damage, armor, and removes fall damage as long as you are at full health and hunger.

</details>



</details>



</details>




<details>
<summary>The Moon</summary>

You ever look up at the moon and think, "I wanna go there?" Well now you can! First look deep underground for an amethyst geode. Amethyst geodes fell from the moon in the most cataclysmic meteor shower the earth had ever seen over 1.2 million years ago. The pressure and heat from entering the atmosphere had transformed the moonstone into calcite, now you have to reverse it. Freeze calcite, release the pressure, and blast smelt it back to it's moonstone form! Once you've done that, craft a teleporter. Teleporters are made with a beacon in the middle, three netherite ingots on the bottom, two redstone blocks on each side, and three amethyst shards on top. Then, craft the teleporter remote, one netherite ingot, one redstone dust, and one redstone torch. Then, place the teleporter on top of the moonstone and link the remote to the teleporter and there you go- your own personal trip to the moon! But be warned- teleportation is tricky, and the technology can't always predict where the ground floor is going to be, be prepared to mine yourself out of a hole!

</details>
